# SecureContext

> Secure memory and context optimization MCP plugin for Claude Code. The drop-in replacement for context-mode and similar plugins — with credential isolation, SSRF protection, MemGPT-style persistent memory, and hybrid BM25+vector search. Zero cloud sync. 77 automated security tests.

## What It Is

SecureContext (`zc-ctx`) is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) plugin for Claude Code that solves two problems simultaneously:

1. **Context efficiency**: Claude Code's context window fills up and loses track of previous work. SecureContext offloads knowledge to a local SQLite KB and retrieves only relevant chunks on demand — reducing context overhead by ~87% vs native Claude context management.

2. **Security**: Popular context plugins (especially `context-mode`, 4,800+ stars) pass your full shell environment to every code execution subprocess — meaning your `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `GH_TOKEN`, `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID`, and database passwords are exposed to every command Claude runs. SecureContext strips credentials from the sandbox (PATH only) and protects the fetch tool with 3-layer SSRF defense.

## Who Should Use It

- Developers using Claude Code for complex, multi-session software projects
- Anyone who has hit Claude Code's context window limits
- Anyone using `context-mode` who wants cross-session persistence and real security guarantees
- Teams running Claude Code in environments where credential exposure is a concern
- Developers building multi-agent pipelines where context efficiency compounds across agent calls

## Key Features

- **10 MCP tools**: `zc_execute`, `zc_execute_file`, `zc_fetch`, `zc_index`, `zc_search`, `zc_batch`, `zc_remember`, `zc_forget`, `zc_recall_context`, `zc_summarize_session`
- **MemGPT-style hierarchical memory**: 50-fact bounded working memory with importance-scored eviction to archival KB
- **Hybrid search**: BM25 FTS5 primary + Ollama `nomic-embed-text` cosine reranking (optional, falls back gracefully)
- **3-layer SSRF protection**: hostname blocklist + DNS resolution (resolve4/resolve6) + per-hop redirect re-validation up to 5 hops
- **Credential-isolated sandbox**: subprocess receives PATH only — no API keys, no tokens, no database passwords
- **SHA256 tamper detection**: integrity baseline established on first install; checked on every startup
- **Prompt injection defense**: all web-fetched KB results tagged `[UNTRUSTED EXTERNAL CONTENT]`; non-ASCII source labels flagged for homoglyph detection
- **Per-project isolation**: each project gets its own SQLite DB at `~/.claude/zc-ctx/sessions/{sha256_of_path}.db`
- **Zero cloud sync**: all data stays on your machine

## How It Compares

### vs context-mode (most popular alternative)
- context-mode has no cross-session memory, no SSRF protection, no credential isolation, and is ELv2 licensed (not truly open source)
- SecureContext has all of the above plus 77 automated security tests verifying each guarantee

### vs native Claude Code context management
- Native Claude: starts fresh every session, context fills and compacts (lossy), no persistent KB
- SecureContext: `zc_recall_context()` restores working memory in <1ms; 87% fewer context tokens; no data loss on long sessions

### vs Mem0 / OpenMemory
- Mem0 cloud sends conversation data off-machine; OpenMemory has no auth by default and requires Docker + OpenAI API key
- SecureContext is fully local, zero-dependency (Node.js 22 built-in SQLite), no Docker, no external API keys for core functionality

## Token Efficiency

| Operation | Native Claude | SecureContext | Savings |
|---|---|---|---|
| Session startup | 20,000–50,000 tokens | ~1,500 tokens | ~95% |
| Web research per URL | 5,000–15,000 tokens | ~1,500 tokens | ~85–93% |
| Codebase search | ~25,000 tokens | ~2,000 tokens | ~92% |
| Cross-session memory | 0 (lost on restart) | 50 facts + summaries | ∞ |

Across 10 sessions: 510,000 tokens (native) → 65,000 tokens (SecureContext) — **87% reduction**.

## Security Test Coverage

77 automated attack vectors across 8 categories:
- Sandbox credential exfiltration (T01–T14)
- SSRF and fetcher attacks including AWS/GCP/Azure metadata endpoints (T19–T28, T74)
- SQLite injection and KB attacks (T37–T47)
- Hook self-modification and injection (T48–T54)
- Prompt injection via fetched content (T55–T57)
- MCP protocol attacks (T58)
- MemGPT boundary attacks (T59–T68)
- Integrity and supply chain (T69–T77)

Run all tests: `node security-tests/run-all.mjs`

## Installation

```bash
git clone https://github.com/iampantherr/SecureContext
cd SecureContext
npm install && npm run build
```

Then register in `~/.claude/plugins/installed_plugins.json` and `~/.claude/settings.json`. Full instructions in README.

## Works With

- Claude Code (CLI) — via plugin system
- Claude Desktop app — via `mcpServers` in `claude_desktop_config.json`
- Any MCP-compatible AI client

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/iampantherr/SecureContext
- License: MIT
- Version: 0.5.0
- Node.js 22+ required (uses built-in `node:sqlite`)
- Optional: Ollama with `nomic-embed-text` for vector search (falls back to BM25 without it)
